entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: The primary cereal crop studied for production optimization in Eastern Amhara, Ethiopia

entities.name: MELKASSA-6Q
entities.description: An improved maize variety used in the improved irrigation and agronomic practice

entities.name: LOCAL MAIZE VARIETIES
entities.description: Traditional maize varieties used in the traditional farming practice

entities.name: EASTERN AMHARA
entities.description: The region in Ethiopia where the irrigation schemes and study were located

entities.name: ETHIOPIA
entities.description: The country where the research was conducted

entities.name: GOLINA1
entities.description: One of the specific irrigation sites where the study was conducted

entities.name: SEDENI
entities.description: One of the specific irrigation sites where the study was conducted

entities.name: SEMI-ARID REGIONS
entities.description: Environmental condition characterized by low and uneven rainfall in Eastern Amhara

entities.name: INADEQUATE RAINFALL
entities.description: The lack of sufficient and evenly distributed rainfall affecting rainfed agriculture

entities.name: FARMER RESEARCH EXTENSION GROUP (FREG)
entities.description: A participatory approach used to evaluate improved irrigation technologies

entities.name: IMPROVED IRRIGATION TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: A set of optimized practices including specific maize varieties, row planting, furrow irrigation, and recommended fertilizer rates

entities.name: TRADITIONAL IRRIGATION PRACTICES
entities.description: Existing farming methods involving local varieties, broadcast sowing, and traditional flooding

entities.name: ROW PLANTING
entities.description: A planting technique with specific spacing of 75 cm x 30 cm used in improved practices

entities.name: FURROW IRRIGATION
entities.description: An irrigation method with specific dimensions and gradient used in improved practices

entities.name: BROADCAST SOWING
entities.description: A traditional seed sowing method used in traditional practices

entities.name: TRADITIONAL FLOODING
entities.description: A traditional irrigation method applied at 12-day intervals

entities.name: NUTRIENT MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The application of specific fertilizer rates, such as Urea and NPS, to optimize crop growth

entities.name: GREEN COB YIELD
entities.description: The measure of maize production output per hectare

entities.name: WATER PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The efficiency of water use measured in cobs per cubic meter

entities.name: NET BENEFITS
entities.description: The economic gain calculated per hectare after costs

entities.name: WATER USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The overall efficiency of water utilization in maize production

entities.name: PROFITABILITY
entities.description: The financial viability and gain resulting from the irrigation and agronomic practices

entities.name: SEASONAL WATER DEMAND
entities.description: The total amount of water required by the crop over a growing season
